qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Generic contextualizer cache key omission enables authorization bypass across request-derived identities
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is in Heimdall, but it concerns generic contextualizer cache-key construction and stale authorization reuse in generic_contextualizer.go rather than the advisory's case-sensitive Host matching in request-context extraction and rule host normalization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies Heimdall's case-sensitive host matching bug. The advisory is rooted in request-host and rule-host normalization before rule lookup, while the only manifest-backed qualifying finding describes a separate generic-contextualizer cache poisoning issue.
evidence: The advisory patch in commit 3d05e56a9e7ef0355f17482b4322054af4e85943 lowercases incoming request hosts in internal/handler/requestcontext/extract_url.go and internal/handler/envoyextauth/grpcv3/request_context.go, and lowercases exact and wildcard rule hosts in internal/rules/rule_factory_impl.go before rule lookup.

The only qualifying finding in the run manifest, generic_contextualizer_cache_authorization_bypass, points to repo/internal/rules/mechanisms/contextualizers/generic_contextualizer.go cache-key generation and forwarded header or cookie omission, not host parsing or host matcher evaluation.
